patch.value:
## Summary

The nightly validation matrix has been **red on `windows-latest` since 2026-06-14**, and the failure count has grown from 5 tests (run 27493008584, commit 647cb9b3) to **373 tests across 30 files** (run 27816467001, commit 87229daa, 2026-06-19). It went unnoticed until the new failure alerting (pm-ehbb / PR #300) began surfacing it. POSIX (ubuntu/macos) and the per-PR `Gates` matrix are all green, so this only reproduces on Windows in the full nightly suite.

`macos-latest` had a *single* failure (`/private/var` vs `/var` TMPDIR symlink canonicalization in `extension-command.spec.ts`); that one is **already fixed** by commit `8bc37a22` ("fix(ci): stabilize extension file-alias realpath test"), which postdates the failing nightly — the next nightly should be green for macOS. This issue tracks the **Windows** failures only.

## Primary root-cause hypothesis (the `SyntaxError` cluster — the bulk of the 373)

The dominant signature is:

```
AssertionError: expected [Function] to throw error including 'EXIT:0' but got 'Invalid or unexpected token'
AssertionError: promise rejected "SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token" instead of resolving
```

~18 unit specs drive **TypeScript source** for coverage by computing a file URL and dynamically importing it, e.g. `tests/unit/cli/cli-main-errors.spec.ts:182`:

```ts
const mainUrl = pathToFileURL(path.resolve("src/cli/main.ts"));
const loaded = (await import(mainUrl.href)) as { runPmCli: (argv: string[]) => Promise<void> };
```

On POSIX, Vitest's transform pipeline intercepts the `file://` specifier and transpiles the `.ts` on the fly. On Windows the specifier is `file:///D:/...`, which appears **not** to be intercepted, so Node executes the raw TypeScript and throws `SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token` at the first type annotation. Every test in those files then fails identically, which matches the fan-out across otherwise-unrelated suites (sentry, search, telemetry, governance, bundle-cli, …).

Candidate fixes (need a Windows runner to verify): import the source via a relative module specifier Vitest can transform (`await import("../../../src/cli/main.ts")`) instead of a computed absolute `file://` URL; or normalize the dynamic-import specifier for win32; or load via Vitest's `import.meta` / a shared helper that is platform-correct.

## Secondary failures (hardcoded POSIX separators)

Independent of the SyntaxError cluster, a few assertions hardcode `/`:

- `expected { pmRoot: 'D:\repo\.agents\pm' } to deeply equal { pmRoot: '/repo/.agents/pm' }` — a resolve test asserts POSIX-joined paths.
- `expected '…' to contain '.agents/pm/schema/types.json'` — error-message assertion expects forward slashes; Windows emits `.agents\pm\schema\types.json`.

These should normalize separators (e.g. compare against `path.join(...)` or `.split(path.sep).join("/")`) rather than literal `/`.

## Affected runs / evidence

- Failing run (Windows, 373/30): https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/actions/runs/27816467001 (job 82318768285)
- First red (Windows, 5/5): run 27493008584 (2026-06-14, commit 647cb9b3)
- Last green: run 27460855284 (2026-06-13, commit fb8209ff)
- macOS single failure already fixed in `8bc37a22`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `windows-latest / Node 20` nightly `Test` step is green (0 failed) on the full suite.
- [ ] The `file://`-URL TS dynamic-import pattern is replaced with a platform-correct approach across the ~18 affected specs (or a shared test helper), verified on a Windows runner.
- [ ] The two POSIX-separator assertions are normalized.
- [ ] No coverage regression (still 100/100/100/100 on POSIX) and no new `any`.
- [ ] A `cross-platform` regression guard is added so a Windows-only TS-import or separator regression fails a *required* (per-PR) check, not just the nightly.
Nightly windows-latest red since 2026-06-14: ~373 tests fail with 'SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token' from file://-URL TS dynamic imports + POSIX-separator assertions

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Filed from a maintainer session on Linux. Root cause is Windows-only and not safely reproducible/verifiable from POSIX without risking the green 100%-coverage suite, so it needs a windows-latest runner to land + verify the fix. Non-blocking for releases: the daily Release workflow is independent of nightly and is green (npm @2026.6.19). macOS's single failure is already fixed (commit 8bc37a22).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Partial progress (PR: auto-release-sentry-gate-window-and-alerting): fixed the two hardcoded POSIX-separator assertions called out in this issue — init-command.spec.ts pmRoot now asserts path.resolve(/repo, /repo/.agents/pm) and schema-command.spec.ts asserts path.join(.agents, pm, schema, types.json) instead of forward-slash literals. Added a win32-simulated regression guard (runtime-schema-path-win32-guard.spec.ts) that mocks node:path to path.win32 so a POSIX per-PR runner catches a schema-path separator regression rather than only the nightly. REMAINING (needs a Windows runner to verify): the file://-URL TS dynamic-import cluster (~18 specs computing pathToFileURL(src/*.ts) — chiefly cli-main-errors.spec.ts) that throws SyntaxError on Windows because Vitest does not transform absolute file:// specifiers there; that bulk and a per-PR windows-latest matrix guard stay open.

value.note: Assert pmRoot with path.resolve not a POSIX literal
Assert schema types.json hint with path.join not a POSIX literal

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Separator fixes + win32 guard shipped in PR #305; file:// TS-import bulk remains open.

patch.value: Assert schema types.json hint with path.join not a POSIX literal
Assert pmRoot with path.resolve not a POSIX literal
Make Sentry window contract assertion resilient to harmless formatting refactors
Assert schema types.json hint with path.join not a POSIX literal
Assert pmRoot with path.resolve not a POSIX literal
Make Sentry window contract assertion resilient to harmless formatting refactors
Reject blank numeric Sentry window values instead of coercing to zero
Assert Sentry gate window args by argument array instead of brittle joined command text

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Evidence: addressed PR #305 review follow-up by making Sentry window contract assertions resilient, rejecting blank --sentry-window-days values, and regenerating CHANGELOG.md from the corrected pm-nb08 source title. Focused test passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/scripts/release/sentry-telemetry-gate.spec.ts tests/unit/scripts/release/run-gates.spec.ts tests/integration/release-automation-contract.spec.ts (67 tests).

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Verification evidence: pnpm changelog:pm:check passed with pm-changelog 2026.6.14 (npm latest also 2026.6.14); pnpm quality:static passed; pnpm security:scan passed; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed with 4689 tests and literal 100/100/100/100 coverage; pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift passed with history_drift ok and only known pre-existing resolution warnings; isolated temp-dir smoke passed create/update/files/test --run/close/validate with no warnings; live Sentry/telemetry gate passed with window_days=14, critical=0, high=0, finish_error_rate_pct=1.87, failures_without_error_code_rows=0.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Dependency/GitHub report sweep for PR #305: gh issue list returned no open repo issues, gh Dependabot alerts returned [], gh code-scanning alerts returned [], pnpm audit --audit-level moderate passed, pnpm security:scan passed. pnpm outdated only reports major updates commander 15.0.0 and @types/node 26.0.0; commander 15 is already evaluated/deferred in pm-7j8t because it requires Node >=22.12 while package.json supports node >=20, and @types/node 26 is a major type-surface bump not bundled into this release-gate review follow-up.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Review follow-up: addressed fresh Gemini current-head finding by making parseNumber treat null and undefined as missing values before blank-string trimming, preserving fallback semantics and avoiding a defensive TypeError path.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Verification evidence after Gemini undefined-guard follow-up: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/scripts/release/sentry-telemetry-gate.spec.ts passed (46 tests); pnpm quality:static passed; pnpm changelog:pm:check passed; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed with 4689 tests and literal 100/100/100/100 coverage.
